evidence.velocity_vs_rigour: ensuring data quality, and creating ETL/ELT pipelines—primarily in Stambia—plus implementing automated data quality checks
evidence.velocity_vs_rigour_explanation: The responsibilities emphasize data quality, automated quality checks, and relational modeling methodology with no velocity language present anywhere in the JD.
evidence.domain_risk: collaborate with business areas to translate functional needs into data models and technical specifications
evidence.domain_risk_explanation: The role serves general business decision-making through dashboards and data models with no explicit financial reporting, regulatory, or compliance language.
evidence.data_team_maturity: designing optimized data models, developing performance dashboards, ensuring data quality, and creating ETL/ELT pipelines
evidence.data_team_maturity_explanation: Named ETL tools (Stambia specifically) appear in responsibilities with an existing methodology (Kimball) in place, indicating an established but still-developing function rather than early-stage or fully mature.
evidence.jd_authorship: creating ETL/ELT pipelines—primarily in Stambia—plus implementing automated data quality checks
evidence.jd_authorship_explanation: Naming a specific, less-common ETL tool (Stambia) as the primary pipeline tool alongside the Kimball methodology shows technical specificity only a hiring manager would include.
evidence.stakeholder_orientation: collaborate with business areas to translate functional needs into data models and technical specifications
evidence.stakeholder_orientation_explanation: The role builds the modeling layer and data infrastructure consumed by business areas broadly, without a specific commercial, product, or finance emphasis, placing it in internal_data.
evidence.autonomy_level: collaborate with business areas to translate functional needs into data models and technical specifications
evidence.autonomy_level_explanation: The role translates functional needs defined by business areas into technical specifications, reflecting scoped delivery rather than independently set direction.
evidence.ai_role_explanation: The JD contains no mention of AI tools or AI-consuming infrastructure anywhere in the text.
evidence.testing_framing_explanation: Automated data quality checks are listed as a core responsibility the candidate implements and owns, making this a responsibility rather than a listed tool.
evidence.loss_aversion_framing: ensuring data quality, and creating ETL/ELT pipelines—primarily in Stambia—plus implementing automated data quality checks
evidence.loss_aversion_framing_explanation: Data quality and automated checks are emphasized as secondary to the core modeling and pipeline delivery work, reflecting operational reliability concern without a full compliance/risk register.
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix: designing optimized data models, developing performance dashboards, ensuring data quality, and creating ETL/ELT pipelines
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix_explanation: The role mixes designing new optimized models with maintaining and improving existing ETL pipelines and quality checks, indicating a mid-maturity function rather than a from-scratch build.
evidence.language_gate: Minimum B2 French and fluent English are mandatory.
evidence.loss_aversion: implementing automated data quality checks — reflects operational concern about pipeline/data failures rather than compliance or stakeholder trust.
